title: PutObject, Metadata, Checksums, and Encryption Defaults · Runtime Contract: Putobject Body Ownership
summary: A service uploads an unbounded reader, uses the returned ETag as a universal digest, loses content metadata on replacement, and cannot prove which bytes S3 accepted. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the putobject body ownership increment in single-object upload and integrity evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Own and close input bodies, preserve cancellation, bound bytes, and distinguish request completion from later business publication.
Expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Putobject Body Ownership · Theory Putobject Body Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 4853 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove accepted bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without using ETag as a universal digest; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
